title: ResolutionGroup
description: Defines a resolution group — the unit of ownership for work items. Every work item must be assigned to exactly one group at all times (domain invariant). Groups control which users see which items: members see items assigned to their group; visibleRoles see all items in the group regardless of membership (for oversight roles like SUPERVISOR). The fallback/default group for a workflow type is declared in routing-config.defaultGroup, not on the group itself, because a group may serve as default for some workflow types but not others.

name.description: Human-readable group name (e.g. Settlement Team - EMEA, SSI Setup Queue, Compliance Review).

members.description: Explicit group members. May be empty if membership is managed externally via LDAP/AD (see membershipSource).

membershipSource.description: STATIC: members array is authoritative. LDAP/AD: membership is resolved from an external directory at runtime; the members array may be empty or used as an override.

visibleRoles.description: Roles that can see ALL items assigned to this group without being explicit members (e.g. SUPERVISOR, COMPLIANCE_OFFICER). Items in this group are NOT visible to all authenticated users — only to members and to roles listed here. Role names must be declared in user-role-config.

maxUnactionedAlertMinutes.description: Alert threshold: fire an alert if any work item has been assigned to this group without a state transition for longer than this duration (in minutes). Required — especially critical for groups used as routing-config.defaultGroup to ensure unrouted items are detected promptly.
